steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.to eat: 'to eat' is 'edere' — 'est' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to know: 'to know' is 'scire' — do not confuse with 'esse'

hints.Future indicative: Future of 'esse' would be 'erit' — 'est' is present
hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sit' — 'est' is indicative

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — 'est' here is from 'esse'
hints.sto, stare, steti, statum: These are the parts of 'stare' (to stand) — different verb
hints.sisto, sistere, stiti, statum: These are the parts of 'sistere' (to set / stop) — not 'esse'

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'legem' — 'Lex' is the nominative subject
hints.Vocative: Vocative would also be 'lex' in form, but context shows this is a definition, not direct address
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'lege' — 'Lex' is nominative here

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'lex, legis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'lex' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'lex, legis' is 3rd declension

hints.Ablative: Ablative singular would be 'ratione' — 'ratio' is the predicate nominative
hints.Dative: Dative singular would be 'rationi' — 'ratio' is nominative
hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'rationem' — 'ratio' is the predicate nominative after 'est'

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'ratio, rationis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'ratio' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'ratio, rationis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: While 'natura' can be nominative, after 'in' it must be ablative (place where)
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing nature — 'natura' follows the preposition 'in'
hints.Accusative: Accusative after 'in' would indicate motion toward — here 'in natura' means 'in nature' (location)

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'natura, naturae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'natura' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'natura' is 1st declension

translation: Law is the highest reason implanted in nature.
